metadata.name: action_outcome_retrieval_quality_labels
metadata.purpose: Query-candidate retrieval quality labels for ActionOutcomeReplay Experiment 004.
metadata.notes: Labels judge whether a retrieved episode should help the next action decision, not whether it is merely similar.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Same deploy-linked checkout timeout family; rollback success is a useful contrast to failed restart.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Similar checkout latency but CPU-driven mitigation is not the same client-pool regression.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Pool wait evidence and config revert provide useful direct-remediation evidence.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: External payment gateway timeout was fixed by traffic drain, unlike the failed scale-out.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Payment errors are similar, but single-pod skew does not match service-wide gateway timeout.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Payment timeout with deploy retry regression gives a useful non-scale-out recovery example.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Scale-out succeeded only because CPU was saturated; query CPU is low and traces point external.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: DB pool saturation recovered through config revert, a useful contrast to failed scale-out.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Inventory DB errors are similar, but one-pod skew does not match service-wide DB saturation.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: DB saturation from bulk writes is a useful non-scale-out remediation pattern.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Scale-out succeeded only because DB connections were not saturated and app CPU was high.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Shared recommendation cache freshness lag recovered through targeted cache purge.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Recommendation staleness is similar, but deploy fallback regression differs from shared cache lag.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Failed scale-out is useful negative evidence for shared cache staleness.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Restart worked only for pod-local cache skew, not shared redis freshness lag.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Cache purge failed only because the true root cause was deploy fallback logic.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Capacity-driven backlog recovered through scale-out, the key contrast to failed restart.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Backlog is similar, but poison-message retry loop has a different remediation path.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Traffic drain protected checkout during broker throttle but does not solve capacity backlog.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Restart succeeded only for one stuck consumer partition, not broad capacity shortage.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Scale-out failed because of poison-message retries, not because scale-out is wrong for capacity backlog.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Restart mitigates memory pressure but is not durable for deploy-wide search leak.

labels.label: useful
labels.reason: Rollback fixed deploy-correlated monotonic RSS growth, matching the durable action needed.

labels.label: superficial
labels.reason: Shard-local traffic drain is useful mitigation but not the same deploy-wide leak.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Restart succeeded only for a single-pod leak without deploy correlation.

labels.label: misleading
labels.reason: Rollback failed because index refresh, not binary deploy, caused the memory spike.
